title: ‘Reading felt impossible’: Why she’s running the Boston Marathon for BPL
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Marguerite Flynn, from Hingham, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Boston Public Library.
Flynn overcame challenges with dyslexia, ADHD, and a rare vascular condition affecting her running ability.
She values libraries as crucial spaces for education and is fundraising to support Boston Public Library's services.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon for her patient
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Amy Comander, 51, from Newton, is running her 13th Boston Marathon in 2026 for the Ellie Fund.
Comander is running in honor of her patient, Christina, who is undergoing treatment for advanced breast cancer.
The Ellie Fund aids breast cancer patients by providing transportation, grocery assistance, childcare, meals, and therapy access.

title: Here’s why a trade for Brian Thomas Jr. would be a home run move for the Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: The New England Patriots are considering Brian Thomas Jr. from the Jaguars as a trade option to boost their roster.
Thomas enters his third NFL season with potential, offering 1,300+ scrimmage yards and 10 touchdowns in 2024.
Acquiring Thomas could provide financial flexibility, allowing the Patriots to address other positions while adding his talent.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Ryan Gosling hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Ryan Gosling hosted 'Saturday Night Live' for the fourth time on March 7, 2026, with Gorillaz as musical guest.
Gorillaz performed their first-ever single 'Clint Eastwood' during the show, celebrating the band's 25th anniversary.
Harry Styles was in the audience for the episode, previewing his dual role as host and musical guest for next week.

title: Crew member on sunken vessel off Cape Cod has died, officials identify him
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Angel Nieves, 37, from New Bedford, died after the Yankee Rose fishing boat capsized off Cape Cod.
The search for a missing crew member has ended due to poor weather conditions near Provincetown.
The Yankee Rose was previously involved in a carbon monoxide incident on February 26, affecting eight people.

title: Patriots reportedly among teams interested in Packers’ leading WR in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots among several teams closely monitoring Packers' wide receiver Romeo Doubs in upcoming free agency.
Doubs logged team-high 55 receptions and 724 receiving yards last season and is ranked 20th among free agents.
ESPN projects Doubs to receive a three-year, $49 million contract with $30 million guaranteed in free agency.

title: NH cold case solved 40 years after police found man’s skull in woods
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A man whose skull was found in Bristol, NH in 1986 has been identified as Warren Kuchinsky.
Kuchinsky's identity was confirmed through DNA testing by the DNA Doe Project, aiding in cases of unidentified remains.
No evidence of foul play was found in Kuchinsky’s death, last confirmed alive in the mid-1970s.

title: Boston police issue scam warning for fake parking violations
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police alert residents about fake parking notices pretending to be from Boston Municipal Court.
The scam uses official-looking documents with a QR code for fraudulent payments, police warn.
Officials urge recipients to verify parking tickets on the city's website and report fake notices to authorities.

title: Mansfield man charged with illegally possessing ammunition, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Michael Kennedy, 48, of Mansfield was charged with being a felon in possession of ammunition, federal prosecutors announced.
Authorities found ammunition, firearm parts, and a loaded pistol in Kennedy's home during a supervised release for past convictions.
If convicted, Kennedy could face up to 15 years in prison and a $250,000 fine, according to prosecutors.

title: Here are four under the radar Red Sox players standing out in spring training
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Braiden Ward stands out with 10 hits and 12 steals in 12 spring training games for the Red Sox.
Max Ferguson leads the Red Sox in RBIs with 7, showing promise with his versatility in positions.
Mickey Gasper and Tayron Guerrero are potential valuable additions to Red Sox's depth in catching and bullpen roles.
